examples.title: Peterson's Solution Pseudocode
examples.description: Standard pseudocode implementation of Peterson's algorithm for two processes
examples.explanation: The algorithm uses two mechanisms: flag array shows desire to enter, turn variable resolves conflicts. The while loop ensures mutual exclusion by waiting if the other process wants in AND it's the other process's turn.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) shared space for 2 flags and 1 turn variable

examples.title: Peterson's Solution in C
examples.description: C implementation of Peterson's algorithm with busy-waiting
examples.explanation: This C implementation creates two threads that compete for the critical section. The while loop implements busy-waiting. Note: On modern multi-core systems, this may require memory barriers for correct behavior due to CPU/memory reordering.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) for shared variables plus O(1) per thread stack

examples.title: Peterson's Solution in Python
examples.description: Python implementation demonstrating Peterson's algorithm with threading
examples.code:
import threading
import time

class PetersonSolution:
    def __init__(self):
        self.flag = [False, False]
        self.turn = 0
        self.iterations = 5
    
    def process(self, process_id):
        other = 1 - process_id
        for i in range(self.iterations):
            # Entry Section
            self.flag[process_id] = True
            self.turn = other
            while self.flag[other] and self.turn == other:
                pass  # Busy wait
            
            # Critical Section
            print(f"Process {process_id} is in CS (iteration {i+1})")
            
            # Exit Section
            self.flag[process_id] = False
            
            # Remainder Section
            time.sleep(0.001)

# Create and run threads
ps = PetersonSolution()
t0 = threading.Thread(target=ps.process, args=(0,))
t1 = threading.Thread(target=ps.process, args=(1,))

t0.start()
t1.start()
t0.join()
t1.join()
print("Both processes completed")
examples.explanation: Python implementation using the threading module. The busy-wait uses a simple pass statement. Python's GIL provides some protection but the algorithm logic still applies. For true multiprocessing, would need multiprocessing module with shared memory.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) shared memory plus O(1) per thread

examples.title: Peterson's Solution in Java
examples.description: Java implementation with volatile variables for visibility guarantees
examples.explanation: Java implementation using volatile keyword for flag and turn variables. Volatile ensures visibility across threads and prevents some reordering, though full memory barriers may still be needed on strongly ordered architectures. The busy-wait loop implements the entry section logic.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) shared volatile memory plus O(1) per thread stack
